name: 06_proxy_speaker
description: Diarization scenario: candidate is identified normally, but mid-interview the diarizer detects a SECOND voice in the candidate's audio stream and the live voice embedding drifts away from the enrollment sample — a proxy is answering the hard questions. Identification must hold; fraud flags must fire.

data.text: Hi Karan, thanks for joining. Could you introduce yourself?
Hi Sophie, I'm Karan. I've been working as a backend engineer for six years, currently at a healthtech startup where I own the appointments platform.
Great. Tell me about a system you designed end to end?
Sure — I designed our notification service. In my previous role we had five channels, so I built a fan-out layer with per-channel rate limits and a preference engine in front.
Okay, let's go deeper. How would you design a distributed rate limiter that survives region failover with exactly-once semantics?

You would want a CRDT-based token accounting layer replicated across regions, with a gossip protocol reconciling grants, and idempotency keys scoped to the failover epoch to preserve exactly-once delivery.
Interesting. And how would you test the epoch reconciliation under partition?
Jepsen-style fault injection against the gossip layer, asserting convergence invariants across partitions while replaying grant histories.
